overall.description: First-person view from a ship's bridge during a stormy night navigation. The scene features operational navigation monitors, a sweeping radar display, and a lightning strike illuminating the rough seas ahead. The footage appears to be from a maritime simulation game.

scene_state.functional_zone: ship bridge navigation station during storm

agents.ego_description: operating the ship's navigation systems while monitoring the radar and forward view during storm conditions

entered.detail: colorful nautical chart display showing coastal map with blue water
circular radar screen with sweeping yellow beam and concentric rings
blue digital display with system data and readouts
metal deck structure of the ship's bow visible through the bridge window
dark turbulent water with visible waves in the distance
metal safety railing on the ship's bow deck

segments.scene_description: View from inside a ship's bridge showing three navigation monitors and the stormy ocean ahead. The radar sweeps continuously as the ship moves through dark, rough waters with low visibility.

events.description: bright lightning flash briefly illuminates the entire bridge and ocean view with intense white-blue light

agents.ego_description: continuing navigation as lightning strikes nearby, briefly lighting up the stormy seascape before returning to darkness

segments.scene_description: A sudden lightning flash at 12 seconds brightly illuminates the bridge interior and the turbulent ocean ahead before returning to darkness. The navigation monitors remain active as the ship continues through the storm.

merged_segments.scene_description: The shot follows a continuous first-person perspective from a ship's bridge as a dark, stormy navigation sequence is momentarily interrupted by a brilliant flash of light. It begins with a view of three active navigation monitors and a sweeping radar display, looking out over dark, rough waters with low visibility. As the ship pushes forward through the turbulent ocean, a sudden lightning strike violently illuminates both the bridge interior and the crashing waves ahead. The scene then shifts back into deep shadow, leaving only the glow of the operational monitors to guide the vessel through the returning darkness.
